mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate equity sell-offs over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving sustained positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline, or 60-day forward returns are negative in >50% of co-occurring expansion months.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during periods of high inflation indicates supply-side bottlenecks that compress corporate margins and depress equity valuations over one month.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines do not predict negative 20-day returns when CPI exceeds 320, or the predictive IC remains below zero across all high-inflation sub-samples.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above the long-term trend signals robust economic expansion that translates into immediate positive market sentiment and higher short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following above-trend GDP prints are negative in >55% of cases, or the signal shows no predictive power in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate hikes that push the policy rate above 5.0% trigger a liquidity contraction that systematically reduces equity risk premiums and generates negative quarterly returns.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in >65% of periods where the funds rate exceeds 5.0%, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that boosts corporate earnings expectations and lifts equity prices over one month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns, or the interaction term loses statistical significance when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales during periods of elevated consumer price inflation indicate demand destruction that forces immediate equity repricing and generates negative weekly returns.
failure_condition: Retail sales declines do not predict negative 5-day returns when inflation is elevated, or the signal out-of-sample accuracy drops below 45%.
